name: Tool Call Correctness
description: Core tool calling scenarios — single tool, multi-param, selection, no-tool, sequencing, unordered

samples.description: Single tool call with integer args

input.prompt: What is 17 plus 25? Use the test_add tool.
input.systemMessage: You have tools available. When asked to perform calculations, use the appropriate tool. Be brief and answer with just the result.

samples.description: Multi-parameter tool call with string and optional args

input.prompt: What's the weather in Tokyo? Use the test_weather tool.
input.systemMessage: You have tools available. When asked about weather, use the test_weather tool. Be brief.

samples.description: Tool selection — should pick multiply, not add

input.prompt: What is 4 times 5? Use the appropriate tool.
input.systemMessage: You have math tools available: test_add for addition, test_multiply for multiplication. Use the correct one. Be brief.

samples.description: Should NOT use any tool — just respond directly

input.prompt: Say hello to the user. Do not use any tools.
input.systemMessage: You have tools available but should only use them when specifically asked to perform calculations or check weather. If the user just wants a greeting, respond directly without tools. Be brief.

samples.description: Sequential tool calls — add first, then multiply

input.prompt: First add 2 and 3 using test_add, then multiply 4 and 5 using test_multiply. Do them in that exact order.
input.systemMessage: You have math tools. When asked to perform multiple operations, do them in the order specified. Use test_add for addition and test_multiply for multiplication. Be brief.

samples.description: Multiple calls to same tool — order doesn't matter

input.prompt: Check the weather in both Tokyo and London using the test_weather tool for each city.
input.systemMessage: You have a weather tool. When asked about multiple cities, call test_weather once for each city. Be brief.
